summary_one_line: Input must be a Mach-O object file whose magic and section structure let bloaty parse a non-empty build_id.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The leading bytes must form a Mach-O magic constant (ce fa ed fe / cf fa ed fe) followed by enough valid Mach-O load-command/section structure (including __ section-name bytes 5f 5f) that file->GetBuildId() returns a non-empty string, so the true side of if (!build_id.empty()) is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe (or cf fa ed fe) and carry embedded 5f 5f (__) section-name bytes and load-command sizing fields; these parse into a Mach-O whose build_id (UUID) is recovered, populating build_id and exercising lines 1751-1757 only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d (WASM \0asm) or similar non-Mach-O magic; bloaty parses them as a format that yields an empty build_id, so GetBuildId() returns empty and line 1750 always takes the false side (T=0 in L).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the object-file format CMPs (magic and structural-field comparisons) and I2SRandReplace splices the exact required literals (Mach-O magic, section-name bytes) into the input at the offsets where the parser compares them; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct the multi-field magic/structure by blind havoc, so they never produce a parseable build_id.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side requires a non-empty build_id at the if guard.
build_id is produced by parsing the object file via file->GetBuildId().
Only winner-resolving seeds exercise the true-side body lines 1751-1757.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1750  T=54 F=0  T=0 F=120  if (!build_id.empty()) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe and carry embedded __ (5f 5f) section-name bytes.
Another winner seed uses the alternate Mach-O 64-bit magic cf fa ed fe.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with non-Mach-O magic such as 00 61 73 6d (\0asm).
At the leading magic offsets W and L bytes are sharply concentrated and disjoint (ce/fa/ed/fe vs 00/61/73/6d).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets, unlike naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; only its vpc counterpart adds it, matching the second decisive pair direction.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 13 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed dcb7ff92fe9d1ffa` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 18 of the ancestor chain. Corroborated with `--fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --seed 40a17ee7fff2f42d`, which showed two dash rows at depths 17 and 18, confirming I2S contribution in both decisive-pair winners under the current floor signal.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a non-empty build_id behind a Mach-O magic/structure check in which naive (no I2S) resolves the branch at the same rate as cmplog within the budget, showing the magic/structure literals are reachable by blind havoc rather than requiring I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: That bloaty's GetBuildId() returns non-empty specifically for Mach-O (vs other formats) is inferred from the seed magic bytes and source guard, not directly observed from a build_id value in the prompt.
